title: Bugsweep bench detection case
description: A single ground-truthed detection case: a known vulnerability in an open-source repo at a pinned pre-fix commit, with the fix commit and the exact ground-truth hunks the harness scores detections against.

id.description: Stable, unique case identifier (kebab/dotted, no spaces).

language.description: Primary language of the vulnerable code (e.g. python, javascript, go).

category.description: Bug category / lens (e.g. sql-injection, path-traversal, race-condition).

cwe.description: Optional CWE identifier for the vulnerability class (e.g. CWE-89).

source.description: Provenance: where the case comes from and the commits that bracket the fix.

repo.description: Clonable URL of the source repository.

pre_fix_commit.description: Full 40-char git SHA of the commit that still contains the bug (the SHA the harness clones and analyzes).

fix_commit.description: Full 40-char git SHA of the commit that fixed the bug.

advisory_url.description: URL of the advisory / disclosure that documents the vulnerability.

disclosure_date.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the vulnerability was disclosed; used to enforce the post-cutoff floor.

ground_truth.description: The scorable truth: which file regions constitute the bug, plus human-readable context.

hunks.description: Line ranges that contain the vulnerability; a detection is credited when it overlaps one of these.

file.description: Repo-relative path to the file containing the hunk.

start.description: First line of the hunk (1-based, inclusive).

end.description: Last line of the hunk (1-based, inclusive).

files.description: All repo-relative files involved in the bug.

fix_summary.description: Human-readable summary of how the fix commit addressed the bug.

size_ceiling.description: Per-case size bounds; the runner SKIPs a clone that exceeds either ceiling to bound context-build cost.

max_files.description: Maximum number of files the clone may contain before the runner SKIPs the case.

max_loc.description: Maximum total lines of code the clone may contain before the runner SKIPs the case.

cross_file.description: True if the bug spans more than one file (used to enforce the cross-file floor in the corpus).
